reasoning: Sterling K. Brown is officially nominated for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series for his role in Hulu's "Paradise", with nominations having been announced in July 2025. Despite being one of the final nominees, expert consensus and pundit commentary firmly position the 2025 race as a two-man contest between Noah Wyle ("The Pitt") and Adam Scott ("Severance"), with Wyle frequently labeled the odds-on favorite. Brown also faces steep competition from previous mainstays like Gary Oldman and Pedro Pascal. Consequently, Brown is a distant longshot. The Polymarket price on Aug 7 (post-nominations) was 2% (0.02), which perfectly aligns with him being technically eligible but factually unfavored. I estimate his chances at 3%, leaving a small margin for a surprise upset.
